**SGSC-AI Intelligence Probe Executed**

Jiangsu Kimtan Thermal Systems Engineering Co., Ltd. exhibits a profound structural anomaly typical of a repurposed Foreign Direct Investment (FDI) shell or a severely downsized manufacturing plant. Established in 2006 as a joint venture with a massive fully paid-in capital of 33.06 million USD, its historical name ('Jiangsu Xin Kimtan Fluorine Chemical') reveals its origin as a heavy chemical plant. It has since pivoted its official scope to 'manufacturing air-source water heaters'. However, physical operations are highly suspect: in 2024, the company reported only 4 insured employees. A team of 4 cannot operate a 33M USD chemical or HVAC manufacturing facility. This entity now functions primarily as a dormant industrial shell, a capital holding vehicle, or a localized procurement/import-export office for mechanical hardware, leveraging its massive registered capital to secure trade credit while outsourcing any actual manufacturing.
